phoneticUs: 藢p蓱藧l瑟藞s瑟nd蓹藢t蓱藧n; 藢p蓱藧l瑟藞s瑟nd蓹t蓹n
examples: Asyndeton and polysyndeton so far have not received adequate attention in rhetorical studies.
There is another case where "and" appears between every two coordinate members in a sentence-it is known as polysyndeton in the rhetoric.
As figures of speech at and above the sentence level, asyndeton and polysyndeton so far have not received adequate attention in rhetorical studies and stylistic analyses.
